message:
test(storage): always log stage-profile summaries in steady-state and append-merge profilers

The two `insert_conversation_tree_stage_profile_tracks_*` tests
gathered detailed per-stage timing data on every iteration but only
emitted the human-readable `profile.log_summary(...)` call when the
`CASS_INSERT_TREE_STAGE_PROFILE` environment variable was set. That
gate was useful when these tests were noisy enough to drown out other
output, but the summaries are now structured single-line records that
land cleanly in test logs and are the primary reason these tests
exist (they're profile harnesses — every run that doesn't print is
wasted work).

- `insert_conversation_tree_stage_profile_tracks_steady_state_remote_reuse`
  drops the `log_profile = dotenvy::var(...)` lookup and the gating
  `if log_profile { ... }` branch, calling
  `profile.log_summary(&format!("remote_reuse_{msg_count}_msgs"))`
  unconditionally for each of the (5, 20, 50)-message sweeps.

- `insert_conversation_tree_stage_profile_tracks_append_remote_source_merge`
  gets the same treatment, calling
  `profile.log_summary(&format!("append_remote_merge_{msg_count}_msgs"))`
  unconditionally.

Behavior change: `cargo test --test ...` runs of these tests now
always emit a `[stage profile] ...` line per (msg_count, iteration)
pair. Net new log volume is small (a few lines per test invocation)
and the lines are valuable for catching steady-state regressions
after frankensqlite or message-batching changes — exactly when these
tests fire in CI.

The two tests still respect the surrounding `CASS_DEFER_LEXICAL_UPDATES`
env override (they pin it to "0" via the `_defer_guard`), so the
profile readings remain comparable across runs.
